LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JESSE MORALES AND KATHERINE MORALES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED FOR THE FOLLOWING REASONS. FIRST, PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1324. SECOND, PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA CIVIL CODE SECTION 1714.10. THAT STATUTE APPLIES ON ITS FACE, AND PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SHOW THAT ANY EXCEPTION APPLIES. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT SHOW THAT THERE IS A REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT THEY WILL PREVAIL IN THEIR CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEYS. THIRD, MANY OF THE PROPOSED AMENDMENTS ARE IMPROPER AND SEEK TO ADD CAUSES OF ACTION THAT WERE STRICKEN BY THE COURT IN RULING ON THE EARLIER DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE. FOURTH, THERE IS OBVIOUS PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANTS. GRANTING THE MOTION WOULD REQUIRE TAKING DEFENDANT'S MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OFF CALENDAR AND CONTINUING THE TRIAL DATE. ADDITIONALLY, IT WOULD CREATE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST BETWEEN DEFENDANT AND DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL SUCH THAT DEFENDANT WOULD HAVE TO ENGAGE NEW COUNSEL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENNA WARD, CSR # 7077 =(302/MJM)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complt
Off Calendar Jan-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ARTHUR LEINWOHL, TEL. NO. (530) 546-8100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. PHILIP S. WARD & ATTY. BECKY CACHIA-RIEDL OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS JESSE MORALES AND KATHERINE MORALES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint is Denied for the following reasons. First, Plaintiffs failed to comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1324. Second, Plaintiffs failed to comply with California Civil Code section 1714.10. That statute applies on its face, and Plaintiffs fail to show that any exception applies. Plaintiffs did not show that there is a reasonable probability that they will prevail in their cause of action against Defendant's attorneys. Third, many of the proposed amendments are improper and seek to add causes of action that were stricken by the Court in ruling on the earlier demurrer and motion to strike. Fourth, there is obvious prejudice to Defendants. Granting the motion would require taking Defendant's motions for summary judgment off calendar and continuing the trial date. Additionally, it would create a conflict of interest between Defendant and Defendant's counsel such that Defendant would have to engage new counsel. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVenna Ward, CSR # 7077 =(302/MJM)
